lesson.title: Exponents
lesson.body: An **exponent** tells you how many times to multiply the base by itself: 3⁴ = 3 × 3 × 3 × 3 = 81. Key rules: **Product rule**: aᵐ × aⁿ = aᵐ⁺ⁿ. **Quotient rule**: aᵐ ÷ aⁿ = aᵐ⁻ⁿ. **Power rule**: (aᵐ)ⁿ = aᵐⁿ. **Zero exponent**: a⁰ = 1 (for any a ≠ 0). **Negative exponent**: a⁻ⁿ = 1/aⁿ. For example, 2⁻³ = 1/8.

rooms.question: Evaluate: 2⁵
rooms.explanation: 2⁵ means multiply 2 by itself 5 times: 2×2×2×2×2 = 32. A common error is multiplying the base by the exponent (2×5=10) instead of repeated multiplication. 64 = 2⁶, showing that each additional power doubles the result.

rooms.question: Simplify: x³ × x⁴
steps: \text{Product rule: add exponents when multiplying same base}
rooms.explanation: Product rule: aᵐ × aⁿ = aᵐ⁺ⁿ. So x³ × x⁴ = x^(3+4) = x⁷. The x¹² answer comes from multiplying exponents (3×4), which is the power rule — only correct when a power is raised to a power.

rooms.question: Simplify: (y²)⁵
rooms.explanation: Power rule: (aᵐ)ⁿ = aᵐⁿ. So (y²)⁵ = y^(2×5) = y¹⁰. The y⁷ error adds instead of multiplying (2+5=7). The y²⁵ error reads the exponents as a two-digit number.

rooms.question: Evaluate: 3⁻² (as a fraction)
rooms.explanation: A negative exponent means reciprocal: 3⁻² = 1/3² = 1/9. The −9 error treats the negative exponent as making the result negative — negative exponents flip to the denominator, they don't make the value negative.

rooms.question: Order these values from smallest to largest:
rooms.explanation: Evaluate each: 2⁻¹ = 1/2 = 0.5, 2⁰ = 1, 2² = 4, 2³ = 8. Order: 0.5 < 1 < 4 < 8. Negative exponents produce values between 0 and 1 (for bases > 1). Zero exponent always gives 1. Each increase of 1 in the exponent doubles the value.

boss.name: The Exponent Archon
boss.taunt: Powers stack. So does your suffering.

questions.question: Simplify: (2x³)(5x²)
questions.explanation: Multiply coefficients: 2 × 5 = 10. Apply product rule to variables: x³ × x² = x⁵. Result: 10x⁵. A common error is adding coefficients (2+5=7) rather than multiplying them.

questions.question: Simplify: x⁸ ÷ x³
questions.explanation: Quotient rule: aᵐ ÷ aⁿ = aᵐ⁻ⁿ. So x⁸ ÷ x³ = x^(8−3) = x⁵. The x¹¹ error adds instead of subtracts. 1/x⁵ would be x⁻⁵ (if it were x³ ÷ x⁸).

questions.question: Simplify: (3a²b)³
questions.explanation: Raise each factor to the power 3: 3³ = 27, (a²)³ = a⁶, b³ = b³. Result: 27a⁶b³. The 9a⁶b³ error applies the power to the variable part but only squares the coefficient (3² = 9 instead of 3³ = 27).
